reasoning: Sharp tones in Vietnamese include the four tones with more dramatic pitch changes: sắc (rising), huyền (falling), hỏi (dipping), and nặng (heavy/low)
Flat tones in Vietnamese include ngang (level/no mark) and ngã (tumbling), which have less dramatic pitch changes

answer: Lục bát consists of alternating 6-syllable (lục) and 8-syllable (bát) lines. Key positions (2, 4, 6 in lục lines and 2, 4, 6, 8 in bát lines) must follow specific tone patterns. Sharp and flat tones must alternate in certain positions. The 6th syllable of the lục line rhymes with the 6th syllable of the bát line. The 8th syllable of a bát line often rhymes with the 6th syllable of the next couplet's lục line, creating continuity between couplets.
reasoning: Analysis of the authentic and corrupted couplets reveals these consistent patterns in Vietnamese lục bát poetry
